question: Do the currently retained mixed-year surfaces justify one unified mixed-year topology class, or a split composition with shared suppression and divergent month leadership?
topology_implications: Shared June-led suppression exists across both mixed full years and is best treated as mixed-year background structure.
The strongest 2023-specific signal is December-led continuation-plus-combined mass rather than June suppression alone.
The current retained mixed-year evidence is too split to justify one unified mixed-year topology class.
No new Edge Topology zone promotion is justified from this follow-up alone.

not_promoted_because: The mixed-year evidence splits into shared suppression overlap and divergent combined/continuation leadership.
The 2023 December-led overlay is real but still observational only and not strong enough to justify a new zone label or stronger authority.

next_admissible_follow_up: If reopened later, ask whether a monthly control-relative surface preserves the 2023 December-led continuation overlay or whether that signal remains annual-shared-shape only.
